mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances; unemployment spikes fail to predict negative equity returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts yield negative equity returns in >50% of cases; interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels above 250 during late-cycle expansions trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation periods consistently produce positive equity returns; inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth correlates with negative equity returns in >50% of observations; short-horizon predictive power vanishes after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 signals abrupt labor market contraction and weakening aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll declines of this magnitude fail to predict negative equity returns; forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with moderate inflation indicates a goldilocks economic environment that supports corporate profitability without triggering severe monetary tightening, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High GDP growth paired with moderate inflation consistently precedes negative equity returns; the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production below 90 during periods of tight labor markets signals supply-side constraints and stagflation risks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Low industrial production in tight labor regimes yields positive equity returns in >55% of cases; regime classification fails to improve predictive accuracy.
